### Post by Jan-Hendrik_Ewers on 2018-08-07
Hello, 

I recently tried out Windscribe, which was fine, but after disconnecting from it my internet is not working anymore.

Here are a few outputs:

route -n
```
Kernel IP routing table
Destination     Gateway         Genmask         Flags Metric Ref    Use Iface
```

cat /etc/resolv.conf
```
# Dynamic resolv.conf(5) file for glibc resolver(3) generated by resolvconf(8)
#     DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE BY HAND -- YOUR CHANGES WILL BE OVERWRITTEN
nameserver 127.0.0.53
```

ifconfig
```
[COLOR=#000000][FONT=&amp]enp3s0: flags=4163<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,MULTICAST>  mtu 1500[/FONT][/COLOR]        ether 08:60:6e:87:0e:a4  txqueuelen 1000  (Ethernet)
        RX packets 0  bytes 0 (0.0 B)
        RX errors 0  dropped 0  overruns 0  frame 0
        TX packets 550  bytes 101828 (101.8 KB)
        TX errors 0  dropped 0 overruns 0  carrier 0  collisions 0

lo: flags=73<UP,LOOPBACK,RUNNING>  mtu 65536
        inet 127.0.0.1  netmask 255.0.0.0
        inet6 ::1  prefixlen 128  scopeid 0x10<host>
        loop  txqueuelen 1000  (Local Loopback)
        RX packets 5424  bytes 348810 (348.8 KB)
        RX errors 0  dropped 0  overruns 0  frame 0
        TX packets 5424  bytes 348810 (348.8 KB)
        TX errors 0  dropped 0 overruns 0  carrier 0  collisions 0
```

cat /etc/network/interfaces
```
# interfaces(5) file used by ifup(8) and ifdown(8)auto lo
iface lo inet loopback
```

ping 8.8.8.8
```
connect: Network is unreachable
```

ping google.com
```
ping: google.com: Name or service not known
```
